wikipedia.title: List of unsolved problems in physics
wikipedia.license_note: Problem titles and grouping follow the English Wikipedia list; HQIV status text is programme commentary, not Wikipedia content.

alignment_cores.name: curvature_norm_combinatorial
alignment_cores.desc: Combinatorial curvature norm N67 = 6^7 √3 from discrete null lattice (Lean OctonionicLightCone)

alignment_cores.name: derived_proton_mass_MeV
alignment_cores.desc: Proton mass anchor formally derived from Lean (DerivedNucleonMass + tuft etc)

alignment_cores.name: lapse_factor_ref_point
alignment_cores.desc: ADM lapse at canonical reference-like point (Lean HQVMetric / HQVM_lapse)

alignment_cores.name: omega_k_at_horizon_self
alignment_cores.desc: Ω_k(N;N) must be exactly 1 at the horizon (Lean theorem omega_k_at_horizon_self)

alignment_cores.name: omega_k_partial_at_reference
alignment_cores.desc: Ω_k at lock-in/reference shell relative to itself (Lean omega_k_lockin_calibration)

alignment_cores.name: reference_m
alignment_cores.desc: Lock-in shell index (qcdShell + lattice steps) — changing this is a major formal shift

alignment_cores.name: so8_dim
alignment_cores.desc: so(8) Lie closure dimension (Lean SO8Closure + triality + GeneratorsLieClosure)

phenomenology_metrics.name: alpha_GUT
phenomenology_metrics.desc: GUT coupling from Lean β-running engine (1/42 in paper)

phenomenology_metrics.name: available_modes_ref
phenomenology_metrics.desc: Combinatorial mode count at reference shell (Lean lattice)

phenomenology_metrics.name: bbn_eta10
phenomenology_metrics.desc: Baryon-to-photon ratio η × 10^10 at BBN/CMB from HQIV first-principles (dynamic shell integrator: curvature shells + vev + binding feedback + seed imprint over QCD-to-lock-in window). Paper script hqiv_dynamic_bulk_bbn.py gives ~6.19782 (not 6.10).
phenomenology_metrics.mainstream_note: Mainstream (ΛCDM): fitted parameter (Ω_b h^2) from BBN light-element abundances + CMB damping tail (depends on baryon density + several other cosmological parameters + initial conditions). HQIV: derived ~6.19782 from axioms + discrete dynamics (no fit).

phenomenology_metrics.name: cmb_birefringence_z
phenomenology_metrics.desc: CMB birefringence β (deg) at now from HQIV (α imprint + self-clock; paper script 0.379 deg). Python current 0.3 from witness. Agrees with obs within ~0.4σ per paper.
phenomenology_metrics.mainstream_note: Mainstream: predicts ~0 (no mechanism in standard inflation/GR) or new physics (axions, parity violation). HQIV predicts specific O(0.3-0.4)° from lattice monogamy α=3/5 and now conditions.

phenomenology_metrics.name: deuteron_binding_z
phenomenology_metrics.desc: Deuteron total binding B (MeV) from horizon network ladder vs AME2020 (with real exp σ). Large |z| is current uncalibrated gap; Arena dynamic terms target reduction.
phenomenology_metrics.mainstream_note: Mainstream: fitted from nucleon-nucleon potentials + ~dozens of parameters in chiral EFT / phenomenological models; not derived from deeper axioms

phenomenology_metrics.name: flatness_tuning_exponent
phenomenology_metrics.desc: Tuning exponent for initial curvature to produce observed near-flat universe today. HQIV dynamics from lattice gives natural small Omega_k(now) ~ paper value 0.0098 (agrees with Planck/bounds).
phenomenology_metrics.mainstream_note: Mainstream GR/ΛCDM (no inflation): |Ω_k| at t~t_Pl must be <~10^{-60} (or 10^{-30} at GUT) or universe not flat today; extreme fine-tuning of initial conditions. Inflation stretches but brings eternal inflation, measure problems.

phenomenology_metrics.name: free_neutron_half_life
phenomenology_metrics.desc: Free n lifetime (beta decay) from G_F^2 m_e^5 |M|^2 scaffold + ladder (isotope_ladder + hqiv_nuclear_spectra). Matches experiment to ~0.16% in current ladder (benchmark).
phenomenology_metrics.mainstream_note: Mainstream: measured to high precision (UCN traps, beams); SM predicts via |V_ud| CKM element + nuclear matrix elements (depends on several measured inputs)

phenomenology_metrics.name: hierarchy_tuning_exponent
phenomenology_metrics.desc: Hierarchy tuning exponent: HQIV derives weak/Planck (and GUT) scale separation from lock-in m~4 + discrete counts without quadratic tuning. Matches paper derivations of m_p, alpha_GUT.
phenomenology_metrics.mainstream_note: Mainstream (SM+GR): m_Higgs / M_Pl ~10^{-17}; quadratic divergences require ~10^{32} fine-tuning of bare parameters (or SUSY to ~TeV, or anthropics). No natural first-principles ratio from axioms.

phenomenology_metrics.name: omega_k_present_now
phenomenology_metrics.desc: HQIV predicted Ω_k at the present 'now' slice (dynamic w/ universe age via m_now electron shell). Small positive value must agree roughly with observations (Planck ~0.001, |Ω_k|<~0.02).
phenomenology_metrics.mainstream_note: Mainstream (ΛCDM): Ω_k0 ≈ 0 (flat universe today); flatness problem — requires special initial conditions or inflation to explain why so close to zero (depends on early universe dynamics).

phenomenology_metrics.name: paper_comparisons_max_abs_z
phenomenology_metrics.desc: Max | (HQIV derived - exp) / published_1σ | across binding energies, half-lives, masses, BBN η, CMB etc. from the master paper-comparison suite. Core Arena 'sigma everywhere' driver.
phenomenology_metrics.mainstream_note: Mainstream: per-observable fits / effective theories achieve |z| << 1 on data they were calibrated to (many free params per sector)

phenomenology_metrics.name: proton_electron_mass_ratio
phenomenology_metrics.desc: m_p / m_e from HQIV resonance ladder + nucleon binding (tuft + SM_GR_Unification papers)
phenomenology_metrics.mainstream_note: Mainstream: measured constant (CODATA/PDG fit to many experiments); no first-principles derivation in SM+GR

phenomenology_metrics.name: tuft_hopf_kappa6_correction
phenomenology_metrics.desc: TUFT Hopf κ₆ = η_paper × γ × C₂ (C2 = lapse conc at lock-in) for advanced mass/binding corrections in papers. Python now mirrors Lean.
phenomenology_metrics.mainstream_note: Mainstream: no equivalent; fitted parameters per sector. HQIV: derived topological correction from Hopf/Beltrami bridge (kappa6, C2 terms).

phenomenology_metrics.name: vacuum_energy_discrepancy
phenomenology_metrics.desc: Vacuum energy density from finite sum ½ N(m) ω(m) over causal modes 0 to m_now (matches paper kirchhoff_finite_mode script). HQIV gives observed small value naturally.
phenomenology_metrics.mainstream_note: Mainstream QFT+GR (Planck cutoff): predicts ρ_vac ~10^{120} × observed (vacuum catastrophe); requires 120 orders fine-tuning or new physics (e.g. SUSY to 10^{-3} eV). No first-principles solution.

sigma_snapshot.note: Protected metrics are Lean↔Python exact witnesses (rel_err = 0). Phenomenology metrics use PDG/Planck/literature error bars; Arena rewards broad reduction of rel_err across many observables ('sigma everywhere'). sigma_weighted mixes large-magnitude witnesses — use per-metric rel_err for physics gaps.

status_legend.addressed: Explicit mechanism or derivation in the published HQIV spine.
status_legend.partial: Direction claimed; quantitative closure or uniqueness still open.
status_legend.reinterpreted: Problem reframed (e.g. dark matter as inertia screening).
status_legend.open: Acknowledged gap; no paper claim yet.

problems.title: Theory of everything
problems.hqiv: HQIV is a patch programme: discrete null-lattice combinatorics + horizon thermodynamics + certified so(8) closure aim to link gravity, gauge structure, and mass readouts on one spine — not yet a single accepted TOE Lagrangian.

problems.title: Quantum gravity (GR + QM consistency)
problems.hqiv: Spacetime is modelled as a locally finite causal order with quadratic null-shell growth; gravity enters via modified inertia and HQVM lapse rather than a finished graviton QFT. Discrete carrier + Lean witnesses are the consistency gate.

problems.title: Black hole information paradox
problems.hqiv: Soft dynamical firewall: information is released through horizon bookkeeping on the auxiliary channel instead of being destroyed at a sharp Planck wall.

problems.title: Problem of time
problems.hqiv: A shell-indexed rapidity dial and ADM lapse compression separate wall-clock evolution from apparent cosmological time; full reconciliation with every GR clock construction is still being spelled out in the variational layer.

problems.title: Interpretation of quantum mechanics
problems.hqiv: Born-rule statistics from a finite measurement layer on the auxiliary field; deterministic local accounting — pilot-wave aligned, explicitly not many-worlds branching.

problems.title: Arrow of time
problems.hqiv: Macroscopic direction from causal order (no closed loops), nonnegative entropy production on finite patches, cooler outer shells, and a certified Lyapunov relaxation of under-filled early shells.

problems.title: Yang–Mills existence and mass gap
problems.hqiv: Millennium-class mathematical QFT existence is outside the HQIV patch mandate; colour confinement is approached via discrete networks, not an analytic mass-gap proof.

problems.title: Dark energy / cosmic acceleration
problems.hqiv: Acceleration read through G_eff(φ) = φ^(3/5) and observer-centric lapse; coincidence with matter density is tied to selecting the present hypersurface, not a fitted ΛCDM extension.

problems.title: Dark matter
problems.hqiv: Galactic dynamics attributed to inertia screening on horizons (SPARC-style readouts) rather than a new stable particle species; Arena tracks flyby/SPARC residuals.

problems.title: Matter–antimatter asymmetry
problems.hqiv: Baryon excess (ETA10) from first-principles dynamic shell integrator (curvature + vev + binding feedback; paper script gives ~6.19782 not 6.1). Not a bolt-on CP parameter. Big mainstream problem: fitted in ΛCDM (Ω_b h² from BBN+CMB, depends on multiple params + ICs); no first-principles from SM.

problems.title: Hubble tension
problems.hqiv: Wall-clock vs apparent age split (51.2 Gyr vs 13.8 Gyr appearance) changes distance–redshift readouts; explicit H₀ pipeline comparison is ongoing phenomenology.

problems.title: Horizon / homogeneity problem
problems.hqiv: Large-scale homogeneity argued from entanglement monogamy on overlapping horizons and discrete shell coupling; not a standard slow-roll inflation field postulate.

problems.title: Three generations of quarks and leptons
problems.hqiv: Spin(8) triality on the octonionic carrier organizes three generations; Yukawa hierarchies read from detuned horizon surfaces (mass ladder programme).

problems.title: Hierarchy problem (gravity vs other forces)
problems.hqiv: All scales descend from single lock-in shell (m~4) + lattice combinatorics + α=3/5; no quadratic UV divergences or bare-parameter tuning. Hierarchy tuning exponent = 0 (natural).

problems.title: Color confinement
problems.hqiv: Confinement built into subatomic network weights and string-tension readouts; analytic proof for arbitrary non-abelian gauge theory is not claimed.

problems.title: Strong CP problem / axions
problems.hqiv: No dedicated axion sector in the published spine; strong-phase alignment remains an open extension point for Arena contributors.

problems.title: Neutrino masses and hierarchy
problems.hqiv: Lepton ladder work exists in pyhqiv but absolute neutrino masses and Majorana vs Dirac nature are not closed in the Zenodo paper series yet.

problems.title: Muon anomalous magnetic moment (g − 2)
problems.hqiv: Not yet a headline comparison in the Arena paper-comparison suite; high priority for new tests with PDG error bars.

problems.title: Galaxy rotation curves
problems.hqiv: Rotation curves from horizon-modified inertia (live pyhqiv.orbital + SPARC catalog tests); residual metric is an active Arena improvement target.

problems.title: Flyby anomaly
problems.hqiv: Inertia screening formula compared to literature flyby residuals in test data; dynamic corrections compete on Arena σ.

problems.title: Cosmic birefringence / large-scale polarization
problems.hqiv: Predicted rotation from α = 3/5 and wall-clock age; paper script ~0.379° vs Planck PR4 0.342±0.094 (~0.4σ). Python uses witness 0.3 (Lean) but computes match via surface_wave_self_clock + quantum_mechanics birefringence_redshift.

problems.title: Proton stability and spin crisis
problems.hqiv: Proton mass anchored at reference shell; spin decomposition from 8×8 composites is programme work — decay lifetime not central claim.

problems.title: Nuclear force and binding energies
problems.hqiv: HorizonNetwork + 8×8 binding pipeline; light nuclei compared with explicit σ in tests (deuteron gap documented).

problems.title: High-temperature superconductivity
problems.hqiv: Condensed-matter mechanism not part of the horizon lattice spine; thermo/allotrope Arena metrics are separate phenomenology hooks.

problems.title: Navier–Stokes existence and smoothness
problems.hqiv: Millennium problem in mathematics, not targeted by HQIV.

problems.title: Abiogenesis
problems.hqiv: Origin of life is outside the physics patch; auxiliary-field paper mentions biochemistry only as future simulation regime.

problems.title: Cosmological constant / vacuum energy problem
problems.hqiv: Finite sum over discrete null-lattice modes 0 to m_now (causal horizon cutoff) for vacuum zero-point ½N(m)ω(m); exact match to paper script. Gives observed small ρ_vac naturally (no tuning).

problems.title: Flatness problem / initial conditions for Ω_k
problems.hqiv: Ω_k(now) small positive ~0.0098 derived from lattice shell integral at current age m_now (dynamic with universe age); within obs bounds without initial fine-tuning.
